insight: Tia Threnody is especially strong in sets with Smorcus because Smorcus prevents overteching; Tia also threatens Cynestra if the opponent plays defensively.
author: masn6811
context: Discussion of P1 opening strategy in a set containing Tia Threnody and Smorcus.

insight: DD/DC is the safest P1 opening in Tia sets because it doesn't over-econ and avoids losing to Tia pressure.
author: masn6811
context: Master player analysis of optimal P1 opening strategy against Tia Threnody threat.

insight: Cauterizer is ideal when paired with Chill or Engineer-synergy units like Antima Comet or Tesla Coil, and provides a way to sink colored resources.
author: shadourow
context: Direct question about Cauterizer situational utility.

insight: Valkyrion does not grant granularity to the opponent because opponents must use the barriers to block; it synergizes well with Cryo Ray, Iso Kronus, and Scorchilla.
author: consensus
context: Clarification of Valkyrion mechanics and synergies in response to misconceptions.

insight: Valkyrion provides 4 immediate threat when purchased, which is more valuable than delayed threat, making it better than Tarsiers as an attacker if you already have tricolor tech.
author: flakmaniak
context: Comparison of Valkyrion and Tarsier as attacking options.

insight: Being half a turn ahead on Animus tech is generally desirable, while being half a turn behind on Blastforge tech is preferable.
author: masn6811
context: Expert guidance on relative tech timing for core economic structures.

insight: Colossus is a fine attacker to buy if you have the tech and nothing else to do with it, but you are rarely in that position.
author: amalloy
context: General assessment of Colossus as an attacker option.

insight: Pre-buying Xeno Executor to soak is moderately efficient; its main value is preserving Wall supply and spending Blue that would otherwise be wasted.
author: consensus
context: Discussion of Xeno Executor as a soaking and economic tool.

insight: Choosing between Tarsier+Forcefield and Rhino for 5GR defense depends on whether Forcefield is better for inflation or whether immediate prompt defense is needed; Rhino is preferable late-game to avoid wasting Red.
author: consensus
context: Analysis of defense unit choices and their situational value.

insight: In Feral Warden sets with P1, DD/DD/DDA/TTC is the best opening for Ferals, winning even without other units; rapid attack purchase triggers opponent Feral Warden tech.
author: jamberine
context: Correction of P1 Feral Warden opening strategy.

insight: Shadowfang requires significant upfront Animus investment; Tarsiers are better until you buy 4+ Shadowfangs, and the opportunity cost of tech and defense usually outweighs the long-term Shadowfang advantage.
author: iminabearsuit
context: Detailed analysis of why Shadowfang is considered weak.

insight: Plasmafier is a very good attacker on par with Cynestra and Shadowfang in efficiency.
author: megasupp
context: Assessment of Plasmafier's standing among advanced attackers.

insight: Aggressive red sets without a 2-cost alternate Drone are fundamentally P2-advantaged; Natural Animus feels too slow and Elyot Animus too risky for P1.
author: flakmaniak
context: P1 perspective on meta-match-ups in red-heavy sets.

insight: P2 fastimus (fast Animus) sets are extremely P2-favored, allowing even significantly lower-rated players to beat higher-rated opponents; presence of units preventing P1 undercuts (like Venge Cannon) makes these unwinnable for P1.
author: masn6811
context: Expert explanation of match-up disparity in fastimus sets.

insight: In sets with Chrono Filter and Wincer, DDE is the correct P1 opening because Chrono Filter and Rectifier timing make Wincer on turn 2 optimal.
author: masn6811
context: Specific high-econ opening for unusual set composition.

insight: Zemora is not base+1 viable; in sets with both Zemora and Cynestra, Cynestra is usually correct because Zemora struggles to find adequate absorbers.
author: consensus
context: Expert consensus on Zemora viability and match-up against Cynestra.

insight: In standard Zemora sets, DD/DC/DDC is the standard P1 opening; DD/DD/DDC is a disaster because it's too slow and gets punished by P2's DD/DDC/DDC.
author: consensus
context: Established meta opening for Zemora-heavy sets.

insight: Cynestra is approximately the best attacker in the game due to efficiency and convenient GGGB cost (requiring fewer drones to generate the necessary Green).
author: consensus
context: Expert ranking of advanced attackers.

insight: Plasmafier has positive self-synergy: high efficiency drives up inflation, which lowers drone value, making Plasmafier even more efficient relative to buying drones.
author: masn6811
context: Economic analysis of Plasmafier's meta-effect.

insight: Iso Kronus has self-synergy: when you already own Iso Kronuses, buying another Iso Kronus is better than buying a Gauss Cannon because it retains value when opponents absorb, whereas Gauss Cannon only helps break through on odd turns.
author: amalloy
context: Technical explanation of Iso Kronus self-synergy mechanics.

insight: Cauterizer's 4 Engineers serve as defensive reserve; when building defense, decide how many Engineers to sacrifice and replace them as part of your defense allocation.
author: .bky_1556
context: Advanced defensive positioning technique using Cauterizer.

insight: Energy-consuming units (like Cauterizer synergies) pair exceptionally well with Tesla Coil because both are balanced around buying Engineers independently—buying them together yields a free Engineer.
author: masn6811
context: Economic synergy between energy-cycling units.

insight: Once you buy Blastforge or Animus, you are mostly committed to spending all Red or Blue every turn; buying a tech structure when you cannot afford to spend all colored mana next turn is generally bad.
author: anesotericman
context: Core resource management principle for economic structures.

insight: The first Blastforge can be an exception to the colored-mana commitment rule if it provides an absorbing blocker better than Wall; it is acceptable to excess Blue for a few turns until defense becomes critical.
author: .bky_1556
context: Refinement of Blastforge commitment principle.

insight: Feral Warden is exceptionally convenient because it functions as either a Wall (tanking 2 damage at full health) or a permanent attacker (at 1 HP), often saving 2+ Wall purchases.
author: consensus
context: Explanation of Feral Warden's flexibility and value.

insight: Inflation is the relative value of resources over time; as inflation increases (early to late game), the return on investment of attackers increases relative to drones because preventing damage becomes more valuable than generating income.
author: .bky_1556
context: Fundamental economic principle explaining resource allocation priority shifts.

insight: At peak inflation (endgame), Rhino becomes a better attacker than Tarsier because the game will end in 2 turns or fewer; the immediate threat is more valuable than delayed threat.
author: .bky_1556
context: Concrete example of how inflation determines unit value.

insight: Tarsiers followed by Electrovores is an efficient way to ramp from 0 to 3+ attack in a single turn because Electrovores can be purchased immediately after Tarsiers without additional tech.
author: .bky_1556
context: Attacker sequencing for rapid damage acceleration.

insight: Planning at least one turn ahead is critical; buying Engineers that won't generate value next turn, or building resources for units you cannot afford before being breached, are common beginner mistakes.
author: consensus
context: Fundamental tactical principle for turn-by-turn decision-making.
